record.field: Rocket.Chat is an open-source, fully customizable communications platform that gives organizations a self-hosted alternative to Slack and Microsoft Teams with full data sovereignty.

Rocket.Chat is an open-source, fully customizable communications platform that gives organizations a self-hosted alternative to Slack and Microsoft Teams with full data sovereignty.

It unifies internal team chat, omnichannel customer engagement, and cross-network messaging in a single workspace you control. Built in TypeScript on Node.js and MongoDB, it can run self-hosted, in the cloud, or in air-gapped environments, and supports Matrix federation to communicate across independent servers. It targets teams and enterprises with strict privacy, compliance, and data-protection requirements.

Key features
  • Channels, private groups, direct and threaded messaging with file sharing and search
  • Audio and video calls, conferencing and screen sharing
  • Omnichannel inbox connecting live chat, email and external messaging apps for customer support
  • Matrix federation to interconnect separate Rocket.Chat and Matrix servers
  • App marketplace, REST/Realtime APIs, webhooks and chatbot integrations for extensibility
  • Web, desktop (Windows/macOS/Linux) and mobile (iOS/Android) clients, with roles, permissions and SSO
Best for

Teams, enterprises and public-sector organizations that need a private, self-hosted collaboration hub with data sovereignty, plus businesses that want to combine internal chat with omnichannel customer communication on infrastructure they own.

services: rocketchat: image: registry.rocket.chat/rocketchat/rocket.chat:6.9.2 restart: always environment: MONGO_URL: mongodb://mongodb:27017/rocketchat?replicaSet=rs0 MONGO_OPLOG_URL: mongodb://mongodb:27017/local?replicaSet=rs0 ROOT_URL: ${ROOT_URL:-http://{{ rocketchat_host }}:{{ rocketchat_port }}} PORT: {{ rocketchat_port }} DEPLOY_METHOD: docker DEPLOY_PLATFORM: null REG_TOKEN: null depends_on: - mongodb container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mongodb: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/mongodb:5.0 restart: always volumes: - ./mongodb_data:/bitnami/mongodb environment: MONGODB_REPLICA_SET_MODE: primary MONGODB_REPLICA_SET_NAME: rs0 MONGODB_PORT_NUMBER: 27017 MONGODB_INITIAL_PRIMARY_HOST: mongodb MONGODB_INITIAL_PRIMARY_PORT_NUMBER: 27017 MONGODB_ADVERTISED_HOSTNAME: mongodb MONGODB_ENABLE_JOURNAL: true ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD: true container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mongodb' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose exec -T mongodb sh -c 'mongodump --archive' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongodump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mongodb sleep 8 docker compose exec -T mongodb sh -c 'mongorestore --drop --archive' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongorestore OK"
